title: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film Jo
display_title: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film Jo
wikitext:
[]: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by Rei IV » Fri Sep 01, 2017 12:42 am Wow. It's hard to believe it's been 10 years since Evangelion: Jo (or 1.0) was released in theaters in Japan. It's almost surreal and feels like it was just yesterday. Hell, it's even hard to believe it's been (almost) 5 years since Q back in 2012! These movies has been very controversial, yes, and hit and miss for audiences (the fans). It's understandable but I I'm one who has enjoyed it greatly and prefer it to the original NGE series (as I saw these movies first before I saw the original) . Despite some glaring flaws and issues, I find the Evangelion films to be solid and well made and I love that it deviates greatly from the original narrative and stresses that This Is (Not) The Eva You Know . I'm looking forward 3.0+1.0, the conclusion to the quadriology. I haven't been disappointed yet and look forward to (hopefully) another solid film. Edited: Replaced the "givers the finger to" to "deviates greatly from" so that it would sound less edgelord.

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 01, 2017 1:22 am "Gives the finger to the original narrative"? Framing things that way makes it sound like the new movies are try-hard edgelords.

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Sep 01, 2017 3:10 am Don't you agree that some ardent apologists for the original might think that way?

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Fri Sep 01, 2017 3:20 am I think it'd be pretty difficult to make anything edgier than NGE, no? (Well, not without seriously going off the deep end.)

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 01, 2017 3:28 am pwhodges wrote: Don't you agree that some ardent apologists for the original might think that way?

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Sep 01, 2017 3:56 am Despite some glaring flaws and issues, I find Evangelion films to be solid and well made and I love that it pretty much gives the finger to the original narrative and stresses that This Is (Not) The Eva You Know.

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by Cybermat47 » Fri Sep 01, 2017 5:45 am Ray wrote: ... the existence of Lilith being public knowledge it's pretty much identical to the original series.

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by Rei IV » Fri Sep 01, 2017 9:04 am Ray wrote: Howso? Eva 1.11 is pretty much functionally Identical to the first six episodes of the original series, (right down to the infamous underwear drawer scene my parents gave me a lecture about). Save some minor tweaks like the sea being blood and the existence of Lilith being public knowledge it's pretty much identical to the original series. I don't understand what you mean by this. If anything, it didn't throw us into the territory of 'this will be different from the series' until 2.22.

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Sep 01, 2017 2:31 pm This quadrilogy has given us 3.0, which is one of the best things in the entire franchise SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Sep 01, 2017 6:35 pm I composed some thoughts on how ten years have passed on my site, it turned out quite nicely, and is worth a read: http://www.evamonkey.com/ten-years-have ... elion-1-0/

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Sep 02, 2017 2:26 pm imprimatur13 wrote: I think it'd be pretty difficult to make anything edgier than NGE, no? (Well, not without seriously going off the deep end.)

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Sep 02, 2017 3:48 pm ...and in that time we've advanced the commentary by 2.5 episodes. At this rate, if we include EoE as 2 episodes, the commentary will complete around the time of EoE 's centenary. Otherwise I have nothing to add that I haven't already said over that span. ElMariachi wrote: I would like to introduce you to an anime that's called Code Geass Lelouch.

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by imprimatur13 » Sat Sep 02, 2017 7:21 pm ElMariachi wrote: I would like to introduce you to an anime that's called Code Geass Lelouch.

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Sep 28, 2017 8:13 am I already posted a link to a blog post with my thoughts about this, however I have since revised it a few times, and I also adapted it into a video. So, if you enjoy listening to me ramble about Eva: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hGgbOLEW4gk

[]: Re: A decade has gone by since the first Rebuild film (Jo)....             Quote Post by ShonHam » Mon Aug 13, 2018 9:53 am It's kind of difficult to think about how large a space of time has passed between the first and final film coming in 2020, but what's even crazier is remembering that this started way back in the 90s dude
